IrriSmart 01 / 12
IrriSmart
A Low-Cost IoT and AI-Powered Precision Irrigation Advisory System for Smallholder Farmers in the Tadla Region, Morocco
Yasmine Kouch | Supervisor: Dr. Amine Abouaomar | AUI Spring 2026
irrismart.up.railway.app
Welcome everyone. I'm Yasmine Kouch and today I'll present IrriSmart — a low-cost IoT and AI-powered precision irrigation advisory system designed for smallholder farmers in Morocco's Tadla region.
IrriSmart 02 / 12
WRI Aqueduct — Water Stress Morocco
Morocco's Water Crisis
85%
of water used by agriculture
45%
dam capacity lost since 2017
30%
irrigation allocation cut 2024–25
Béni Mellal-Khénifra | 105,500 irrigated hectares | 7 consecutive drought years | ORMVAT perimeter
Morocco faces a severe water crisis. Agriculture consumes 85% of available water, yet dam capacity has fallen 45% since 2017 and irrigation allocations were cut 30% in 2024–25. The Béni Mellal-Khénifra region alone has endured seven consecutive drought years.
IrriSmart 03 / 13
The Problem IrriSmart Solves
The Knowledge Exists
FAO-56 Penman-Monteith ET₀ models
INRA Tadla monthly soil baselines
Open-Meteo 7-day free forecasts
But Doesn't Reach Farmers
Interfaces in formal French only
No affordable rural hardware
No Darija language support
Fixed-schedule irrigation wastes water
The knowledge to optimise irrigation exists — FAO-56 ET₀ models, INRA soil baselines, free weather forecasts. The problem is that this knowledge never reaches the farmer. Interfaces are in formal French, hardware is unaffordable, and there is no Darija support.
IrriSmart 04 / 12
Three-Tier System Architecture
Field Tier
IoT Sensing
Cloud Tier
ML Inference
User Tier
Dashboard + SMS + Chatbot
IrriSmart addresses this through a three-tier architecture: field-level IoT sensors, a cloud ML inference engine, and a user interface delivered via web dashboard, SMS, and a Darija chatbot.
IrriSmart 05 / 12
Solving the Training Data Problem
The Problem
No labelled field data exists
Cannot wait seasons for real data
New deployment = cold start
Physics-Informed Synthetic Data
36,000 samples (12,000 per crop)
FAO-56 Penman-Monteith ET₀ computation
INRA Tadla seasonal moisture baselines
5% random label noise injection
Béni Mellal-Khénifra climate statistics
ET₀ = [Rn − G] + γ[900/(T+273)]·u₂·(eₛ−eₐ) / Δ + γ(1 + 0.34·u₂)  —  FAO-56 Penman-Monteith
We had no labelled field data. Rather than waiting seasons, we generated 36,000 physics-informed synthetic samples using the FAO-56 Penman-Monteith equation combined with INRA Tadla seasonal baselines and 5% label noise for realism.
IrriSmart 06 / 12
Machine Learning Engine
CropAccuracyF1ROC AUC
Olive94.71%0.94230.9503
Citrus94.42%0.94620.9482
Wheat95.00%0.93900.9514
Mean94.71%0.94250.9500
200 estimators  |  max_depth=12  |  class_weight=balanced  |  5-fold CV F1 ≥ 0.93
Our Random Forest classifier achieves a mean test accuracy of 94.71% and a mean ROC AUC of 0.95 across three crops — olive, citrus, and wheat — validated with 5-fold cross-validation.
IrriSmart 07 / 12
Dual-Layer Anomaly Detection
Layer 1 — IsolationForest
4-feature vector: moisture, temperature, humidity, ET₀
contamination=0.05, 200 estimators
Score < −0.25 → critical  |  0 to −0.25 → warning
Sub-millisecond inference, no labelled data required
↓   Sequential detection pipeline   ↓
Layer 2 — Seasonal Z-Score
36 INRA Tadla monthly baselines (3 crops × 12 months)
|z| ≥ 1.5 → moderate amber  |  |z| ≥ 3.0 → critical
April 2026 example: Wheat ID010003, z=1.13, below baseline
Anomaly detection runs in two sequential layers: IsolationForest for real-time sub-millisecond scoring, followed by a seasonal Z-score check against 36 INRA monthly baselines. Together they catch both sudden sensor faults and gradual drift.
IrriSmart 08 / 12
Weather-Integrated Decision Logic
NO ACTION
Rain > 10mm / 48h
WAIT
Forecast ≥ 5mm tomorrow
IRRIGATE
Moisture < threshold AND no rain
The decision engine integrates live weather forecasts. If significant rain is expected, the system defers irrigation automatically — avoiding unnecessary water use while protecting crops.
IrriSmart 09 / 12
Bilingual Interface — French, Arabic, Darija
French Dashboard

French dashboard
Arabic RTL Dashboard
INSERT: Figure 8.11
Arabic RTL dashboard
Darija Chatbot
INSERT: Figure 9.3
Darija chatbot
The only precision irrigation platform with full support in Darija
IrriSmart is the only precision irrigation platform with full Moroccan Darija support, alongside French and Arabic RTL interfaces, dramatically lowering the barrier to adoption.
IrriSmart 10 / 12
Alert System & SMS Notifications
TypeSeverityTrigger
LOW_MOISTURECriticalMoisture below crop threshold
HIGH_TEMPWarningTemperature exceeds upper bound
FROST_WARNINGWarningTemp forecast < 2°C
LOW_BATTERYWarningBattery level < 20%
RAIN_EXPECTEDInfoForecast ≥ 5mm in 24h
SENSOR_OFFLINECriticalNo reading > 30 min
TREND_CHANGEInfoZ-score crosses baseline threshold
INSERT: Twilio SMS screenshot
SENSOR_OFFLINE alert
Alerts received during bench testing — trial prefix removed in production.
The alert system covers seven event types from critical sensor failures to informational rain forecasts, delivered via Twilio SMS — tested successfully in bench conditions.
IrriSmart 11 / 12
Results
94.71%
Mean Test Accuracy
0.95
Mean ROC AUC
40,500L
Water Saved (bench sim)
180
Irrigation Sessions Deferred
CropMoisture OnlyFull 11 FeaturesGain
Olive87.58%94.46%+6.87%
Citrus89.33%95.42%+6.08%
Wheat76.50%94.96%+18.46%
*Water savings derived from bench-test simulation using ORMVAT benchmark rates. Field validation scheduled as immediate future work.
Key results: 94.71% accuracy, 0.95 ROC AUC, 40,500 litres saved and 180 irrigation sessions deferred in bench simulation. The ablation study shows feature engineering delivers an 18.46% accuracy gain for wheat.

Live System
irrismart.up.railway.app
1.Field validation — Béni Mellal-Khénifra soil samples
2.LoRaWAN end-to-end deployment (2–15 km range)

4.Fine-tuned Darija language model
Intelligence agronomique pour les agriculteurs du Tadla
The live system is already deployed at irrismart.up.railway.app. Next steps are field validation with real soil samples, LoRaWAN hardware deployment, ORMVAT perimeter integration, and a fine-tuned Darija language model. Thank you — I'm happy to take questions.
